157: /-! ## Single-Peaked Preferences
158: 
159: Reference: Black (1948), "On the Rationale of Group Decision-making"
160: 
161: A preference ordering over a linearly ordered set is single-peaked if there
162: exists a unique most-preferred alternative (the peak) and preferences decline
163: monotonically away from the peak in both directions.
164: -/
165: 
166: section SinglePeaked
167: 
168: variable [LinearOrder σ]
169: 
170: /-- The peak of a preference relation: the unique strictly most-preferred element -/
171: def is_peak (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
172:   ∀ x, x ≠ p → P R p x
173: 
174: /-- R is single-peaked with peak p on a linearly ordered set.
175:     1. p is the peak (strictly preferred to everything else)
176:     2. Left of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (a ≤ b ≤ p → R b a)
177:     3. Right of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (p ≤ a ≤ b → R a b) -/
178: def single_peaked (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
179:   is_peak R p ∧
180:   (∀ a b, a ≤ b → b ≤ p → R b a) ∧
181:   (∀ a b, p ≤ a → a ≤ b → R a b)
182: 
183: /-- The peak is unique -/
184: theorem single_peaked_peak_unique {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p q : σ}
185:     (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hsq : single_peaked R q) (hne : p ≠ q) : False := by
186:   have hPpq : P R p q := hsp.1 q hne.symm
187:   have hPqp : P R q p := hsq.1 p hne
188:   exact hPpq.2 hPqp.1
189: 
190: /-- Single-peaked implies the peak is the best element (under Reflexive R) -/
191: theorem single_peaked_peak_best {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p : σ} {S : Finset σ}
192:     (hrefl : Reflexive R) (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hpS : p ∈ S) :
193:     is_best_element p S R := by
194:   intro y hy
195:   by_cases heq : y = p
196:   · subst heq; exact hrefl y
197:   · exact (hsp.1 y heq).1
198: 
199: /-- A profile is single-peaked if every voter has single-peaked preferences -/
200: def single_peaked_profile (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ) : Prop :=
201:   ∀ i : ι, single_peaked (prof i).rel (peaks i)
202: 
203: /-! ## Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)
204: 
205: For an odd number of voters with single-peaked preferences over a linearly
206: ordered set, the median peak (the middle element of sorted peaks) is a
207: Condorcet winner under majority rule.
208: 
209: Proof sketch:
210: - For any y < median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak >= median,
211:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
212: - For any y > median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak <= median,
213:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
214: -/
215: 
216: /-- Sorted list of peaks (with duplicates preserved) -/
217: noncomputable def sorted_peaks_list (peaks : ι → σ) : List σ :=
218:   (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).mergeSort (· ≤ ·)
219: 
220: /-- The median peak: the middle element of sorted peaks.
221:     For odd n, this is the unique middle element. -/
222: noncomputable def median_peak [Inhabited σ] (peaks : ι → σ) : σ :=
223:   let s := sorted_peaks_list peaks
224:   s.getD (s.length / 2) default
225: 
226: /-- **Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)**: For an odd number of voters with
227:     single-peaked preferences, the median peak is a Condorcet winner. -/

attributes.gen_ai.system_instructions:
[{"type": "text", "content": "Genere des tactiques Lean 4 et soumet-les au verificateur.

OUTILS PRINCIPAUX:
1. get_proof_state() → but courant + lemmes du SearchAgent + plan du Coordinator
2. file_read_lines(start, end) → lit le contexte autour du sorry (declaration, hyps)
3. compile_probe_goal(line) → SI tu as besoin de re-extraire le but (rare)
4. generate_tactics(goal) → suggestions tactiques heuristiques
5. submit_tactic(tactic) → tente UNE tactique complete (fait l'edit + lake build)
6. submit_decomposition(name, goal, sub_proof, main_proof) → introduit `have`
7. compile() → check apres edit (build SUCCESS + sorry delta + axioms)

STRATEGIE D'ATTAQUE (du simple au structurel):
1. Une-shot: rfl, decide, omega, simp, exact <lemme>
2. Adaptation: simp [<lemme>], exact <lemme> _, rw [<lemme>]
3. Combinaison: constructor + sub-tactiques, refine ⟨_, _⟩, cases/induction
4. Decomposition: si la preuve excede 4-5 lignes ou si le but est complexe,
   utilise submit_decomposition pour creer un `have h : sub := by ...` et
   reduire le but principal. PROGRES STRUCTUREL = sorry qui se rapproche
   d'un sous-but plus simple, meme si le compteur ne baisse pas immediatement.

QUAND UTILISER submit_decomposition:
- Conjonctions, equivalences, quantificateurs imbriques
- Buts arithmetiques avec sous-bornes a etablir
- Counting / cardinalite necessitant transfer (Finset → List → countP)
- Quand 2 essais one-shot ont echoue : DECOMPOSE plutot que d'iterer

CYCLE D'ITERATION (si echec):
- Lire l'erreur compilateur (elle est explicite)
- Adapter (ajouter named arg, changer un lemme, ajouter un cast)
- Si 2 adaptations identiques echouent : DECOMPOSER ou demander plus de lemmes
- Si 3 echecs cumules sur le meme sorry : laisser le Critic / Coordinator reformuler

INTERDIT (verifie toujours dans FAILED ATTEMPTS):
- Repeter une tactique deja en echec (meme texte exact)
- Utiliser sorry sur du code metier existant (anti-regression)
- Modifier le theoreme cible ou ses hypotheses pour faire passer la preuve

FIX PATTERNS GENERAUX (heuristiques, pas hard-codes):
- "type mismatch" → caster (norm_cast / push_cast / Nat.cast_*) ou ajouter `show`
- "unsolved goals" → la tactique laisse des sous-buts → enchainer ou decomposer
- "unfold failed" → la def est opaque → utiliser show ou unfold explicite
- "function expected" → arg implicite manquant, ajouter (a := ...) (b := ...)
- "omega failed" → l'expression n'est pas Nat/Int pure → caster d'abord

IMPORTANT — TON OBJECTIF:
Tu construis une preuve INCREMENTALEMENT. Chaque sous-but qui compile est un
gain meme si le sorry global subsiste. Ne te bloque pas en cherchant le coup
parfait : essaie, lis l'erreur, adapte. Le harnais te donne le retour en
quelques secondes."}]

attributes.gen_ai.tool.description:
Replace the sorry at a given line with a tactic. Auto-detects indentation.

        When `build_check=True` (default), runs lake build after writing and
        reverts the file if compilation fails. The autonomous prover loop also
        does periodic build checks, but inline validation here catches stubs
        like `exact _` immediately and gives the agent a fresh diagnostic.

31: variable {ι σ : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ]
32: 
33: /-! ## Margin Function -/
34: 
35: /-- The margin of x over y in a profile:
36:     |{voters strictly preferring x to y}| - |{voters strictly preferring y to x}|
37:     Positive margin means x beats y in pairwise comparison. -/
38: noncomputable def margin (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : ℤ :=
39:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x y) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
40:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel y x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
41:   ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x y) Finset.univ).card : ℤ) -
42:     ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel y x) Finset.univ).card : ℤ)
43: 
44: /-- Positive margin: x beats y in pairwise comparison -/
45: def margin_pos (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : Prop :=
46:   0 < margin prof x y
47: 
48: /-! ## Condorcet Concepts -/
49: 
50: /-- A Condorcet winner: strictly beats every other alternative in pairwise comparison -/
51: def condorcet_winner (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
52:   x ∈ S ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof x y
53: 
54: /-- A Condorcet loser: strictly loses to every other alternative -/
55: def condorcet_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
56:   x ∈ S ∧ S.card ≥ 2 ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof y x
57: 
58: /-! ## Social Choice Correspondence -/
59: 
60: /-- A Social Choice Correspondence: maps profiles and alternative sets to chosen alternatives -/
61: def SCC (ι σ : Type*) [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ] :=
62:   (ι → PrefOrder σ) → Finset σ → Finset σ
63: 
64: /-! ## Voting Criteria -/
65: 
66: /-- Condorcet criterion: every Condorcet winner is selected -/
67: def condorcet_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
68:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_winner prof S x → x ∈ f prof S
69: 
70: /-- Condorcet loser criterion: no Condorcet loser is selected -/
71: def condorcet_loser_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
72:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_loser prof S x → x ∉ f prof S
73: 
74: /-- Pareto efficiency: if all voters prefer x to y, then y is not selected -/
75: def pareto_scc (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
76:   ∀ prof S x y, x ∈ S → y ∈ S →
77:     (∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) → y ∉ f prof S
78: 
79: /-- Monotonicity: improving x's position preserves x's selection -/
80: def monotonicity (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
81:   ∀ prof prof' S x,
82:     x ∈ f prof S → x ∈ S →
83:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, P (prof i).rel x y → P (prof' i).rel x y) →
84:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, ¬P (prof i).rel y x → ¬P (prof' i).rel y x) →
85:     x ∈ f prof' S
86: 
87: /-! ## Margin Properties -/
88: 
89: theorem margin_antisymm (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) :
90:     margin prof x y = - (margin prof y x) := by
91:   unfold margin
92:   ring
93: 
94: theorem margin_self (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x : σ) :
95:     margin prof x x = 0 := by
96:   unfold margin
97:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
98:   have hN : ∀ i : ι, ¬P (prof i).rel x x := fun _ => false_of_P_self
99:   have h_empty : Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x x) Finset.univ = ∅ := by
100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi"}]}, {"role": "assistant", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "name": "file_read_lines", "arguments": "{"start":100,"end":250}"}]}, {"role": "tool", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call_response", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "response": "100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi

103: theorem margin_pos_iff_neg_rev (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ} :
104:     margin_pos prof x y ↔ margin prof y x < 0 := by

106:   rw [margin_antisymm prof]

109: /-! ## Condorcet Properties -/
110: 
111: /-- The Condorcet winner is unique (when it exists) -/
112: theorem condorcet_winner_unique (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x y : σ}
113:     (hx : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hy : condorcet_winner prof S y) (hxy : x ≠ y) :

118:   have h₂ := hbeat' x hxs hxy
119:   unfold margin_pos at h₁ h₂

121:   linarith

123: /-- A Condorcet winner cannot be a Condorcet loser -/
124: theorem condorcet_winner_not_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x : σ}

157: /-! ## Single-Peaked Preferences
158: 
159: Reference: Black (1948), "On the Rationale of Group Decision-making"
160: 
161: A preference ordering over a linearly ordered set is single-peaked if there
162: exists a unique most-preferred alternative (the peak) and preferences decline
163: monotonically away from the peak in both directions.
164: -/
165: 
166: section SinglePeaked
167: 
168: variable [LinearOrder σ]
169: 
170: /-- The peak of a preference relation: the unique strictly most-preferred element -/
171: def is_peak (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
172:   ∀ x, x ≠ p → P R p x
173: 
174: /-- R is single-peaked with peak p on a linearly ordered set.
175:     1. p is the peak (strictly preferred to everything else)
176:     2. Left of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (a ≤ b ≤ p → R b a)
177:     3. Right of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (p ≤ a ≤ b → R a b) -/
178: def single_peaked (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
179:   is_peak R p ∧
180:   (∀ a b, a ≤ b → b ≤ p → R b a) ∧
181:   (∀ a b, p ≤ a → a ≤ b → R a b)
182: 
183: /-- The peak is unique -/
184: theorem single_peaked_peak_unique {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p q : σ}
185:     (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hsq : single_peaked R q) (hne : p ≠ q) : False := by
186:   have hPpq : P R p q := hsp.1 q hne.symm
187:   have hPqp : P R q p := hsq.1 p hne
188:   exact hPpq.2 hPqp.1
189: 
190: /-- Single-peaked implies the peak is the best element (under Reflexive R) -/
191: theorem single_peaked_peak_best {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p : σ} {S : Finset σ}
192:     (hrefl : Reflexive R) (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hpS : p ∈ S) :
193:     is_best_element p S R := by
194:   intro y hy
195:   by_cases heq : y = p
196:   · subst heq; exact hrefl y
197:   · exact (hsp.1 y heq).1
198: 
199: /-- A profile is single-peaked if every voter has single-peaked preferences -/
200: def single_peaked_profile (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ) : Prop :=
201:   ∀ i : ι, single_peaked (prof i).rel (peaks i)
202: 
203: /-! ## Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)
204: 
205: For an odd number of voters with single-peaked preferences over a linearly
206: ordered set, the median peak (the middle element of sorted peaks) is a
207: Condorcet winner under majority rule.
208: 
209: Proof sketch:
210: - For any y < median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak >= median,
211:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
212: - For any y > median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak <= median,
213:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
214: -/
215: 
216: /-- Sorted list of peaks (with duplicates preserved) -/
217: noncomputable def sorted_peaks_list (peaks : ι → σ) : List σ :=
218:   (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).mergeSort (· ≤ ·)
219: 
220: /-- The median peak: the middle element of sorted peaks.
221:     For odd n, this is the unique middle element. -/
222: noncomputable def median_peak [Inhabited σ] (peaks : ι → σ) : σ :=
223:   let s := sorted_peaks_list peaks
224:   s.getD (s.length / 2) default
225: 
226: /-- **Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)**: For an odd number of voters with
227:     single-peaked preferences, the median peak is a Condorcet winner. -/
228: theorem median_voter_theorem (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ)
229:     (hsp : single_peaked_profile prof peaks)

attributes.gen_ai.system_instructions:
[{"type": "text", "content": "Genere des tactiques Lean 4 et soumet-les au verificateur.

OUTILS PRINCIPAUX:
1. get_proof_state() → but courant + lemmes du SearchAgent + plan du Coordinator
2. file_read_lines(start, end) → lit le contexte autour du sorry (declaration, hyps)
3. compile_probe_goal(line) → SI tu as besoin de re-extraire le but (rare)
4. generate_tactics(goal) → suggestions tactiques heuristiques
5. submit_tactic(tactic) → tente UNE tactique complete (fait l'edit + lake build)
6. submit_decomposition(name, goal, sub_proof, main_proof) → introduit `have`
7. compile() → check apres edit (build SUCCESS + sorry delta + axioms)

STRATEGIE D'ATTAQUE (du simple au structurel):
1. Une-shot: rfl, decide, omega, simp, exact <lemme>
2. Adaptation: simp [<lemme>], exact <lemme> _, rw [<lemme>]
3. Combinaison: constructor + sub-tactiques, refine ⟨_, _⟩, cases/induction
4. Decomposition: si la preuve excede 4-5 lignes ou si le but est complexe,
   utilise submit_decomposition pour creer un `have h : sub := by ...` et
   reduire le but principal. PROGRES STRUCTUREL = sorry qui se rapproche
   d'un sous-but plus simple, meme si le compteur ne baisse pas immediatement.

QUAND UTILISER submit_decomposition:
- Conjonctions, equivalences, quantificateurs imbriques
- Buts arithmetiques avec sous-bornes a etablir
- Counting / cardinalite necessitant transfer (Finset → List → countP)
- Quand 2 essais one-shot ont echoue : DECOMPOSE plutot que d'iterer

CYCLE D'ITERATION (si echec):
- Lire l'erreur compilateur (elle est explicite)
- Adapter (ajouter named arg, changer un lemme, ajouter un cast)
- Si 2 adaptations identiques echouent : DECOMPOSER ou demander plus de lemmes
- Si 3 echecs cumules sur le meme sorry : laisser le Critic / Coordinator reformuler

2:   Social Choice Theory - Voting Definitions
3:   ==========================================

31: variable {ι σ : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ]
32: 
33: /-! ## Margin Function -/
34: 
35: /-- The margin of x over y in a profile:
36:     |{voters strictly preferring x to y}| - |{voters strictly preferring y to x}|
37:     Positive margin means x beats y in pairwise comparison. -/
38: noncomputable def margin (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : ℤ :=
39:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x y) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
40:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel y x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
41:   ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x y) Finset.univ).card : ℤ) -
42:     ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel y x) Finset.univ).card : ℤ)
43: 
44: /-- Positive margin: x beats y in pairwise comparison -/
45: def margin_pos (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : Prop :=
46:   0 < margin prof x y
47: 
48: /-! ## Condorcet Concepts -/
49: 
50: /-- A Condorcet winner: strictly beats every other alternative in pairwise comparison -/
51: def condorcet_winner (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
52:   x ∈ S ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof x y
53: 
54: /-- A Condorcet loser: strictly loses to every other alternative -/
55: def condorcet_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
56:   x ∈ S ∧ S.card ≥ 2 ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof y x
57: 
58: /-! ## Social Choice Correspondence -/
59: 
60: /-- A Social Choice Correspondence: maps profiles and alternative sets to chosen alternatives -/
61: def SCC (ι σ : Type*) [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ] :=
62:   (ι → PrefOrder σ) → Finset σ → Finset σ
63: 
64: /-! ## Voting Criteria -/
65: 
66: /-- Condorcet criterion: every Condorcet winner is selected -/
67: def condorcet_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
68:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_winner prof S x → x ∈ f prof S
69: 
70: /-- Condorcet loser criterion: no Condorcet loser is selected -/
71: def condorcet_loser_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
72:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_loser prof S x → x ∉ f prof S
73: 
74: /-- Pareto efficiency: if all voters prefer x to y, then y is not selected -/
75: def pareto_scc (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
76:   ∀ prof S x y, x ∈ S → y ∈ S →
77:     (∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) → y ∉ f prof S
78: 
79: /-- Monotonicity: improving x's position preserves x's selection -/
80: def monotonicity (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
81:   ∀ prof prof' S x,
82:     x ∈ f prof S → x ∈ S →
83:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, P (prof i).rel x y → P (prof' i).rel x y) →
84:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, ¬P (prof i).rel y x → ¬P (prof' i).rel y x) →
85:     x ∈ f prof' S
86: 
87: /-! ## Margin Properties -/
88: 
89: theorem margin_antisymm (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) :
90:     margin prof x y = - (margin prof y x) := by
91:   unfold margin
92:   ring
93: 
94: theorem margin_self (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x : σ) :
95:     margin prof x x = 0 := by

103: theorem margin_pos_iff_neg_rev (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ} :

109: /-! ## Condorcet Properties -/
110: 
111: /-- The Condorcet winner is unique (when it exists) -/
112: theorem condorcet_winner_unique (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x y : σ}
113:     (hx : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hy : condorcet_winner prof S y) (hxy : x ≠ y) :

118:   have h₂ := hbeat' x hxs hxy

120:   have := margin_antisymm prof x y
121:   linarith
122: 
123: /-- A Condorcet winner cannot be a Condorcet loser -/
124: theorem condorcet_winner_not_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x : σ}
125:     (hw : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hl : condorcet_loser prof S x) :
126:     False := by

128:   obtain ⟨_, hbeat⟩ := hw
129:   obtain ⟨y, hyS, hxy⟩ := exists_second_distinct_mem (by omega : 2 ≤ S.card) hxs
130:   have h₁ := hbeat y hyS hxy
131:   have h₂ := hlose y hyS hxy

133:   have := margin_antisymm prof x y

136: /-! ## Unanimity and Margin -/

138: /-- If all voters strictly prefer x to y, the margin is positive -/
139: theorem margin_pos_of_unanimous [Nonempty ι] (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ}

157: /-! ## Single-Peaked Preferences
158: 
159: Reference: Black (1948), "On the Rationale of Group Decision-making"
160: 
161: A preference ordering over a linearly ordered set is single-peaked if there
162: exists a unique most-preferred alternative (the peak) and preferences decline
163: monotonically away from the peak in both directions.
164: -/
165: 
166: section SinglePeaked
167: 
168: variable [LinearOrder σ]
169: 
170: /-- The peak of a preference relation: the unique strictly most-preferred element -/
171: def is_peak (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
172:   ∀ x, x ≠ p → P R p x
173: 
174: /-- R is single-peaked with peak p on a linearly ordered set.
175:     1. p is the peak (strictly preferred to everything else)
176:     2. Left of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (a ≤ b ≤ p → R b a)
177:     3. Right of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (p ≤ a ≤ b → R a b) -/
178: def single_peaked (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
179:   is_peak R p ∧
180:   (∀ a b, a ≤ b → b ≤ p → R b a) ∧
181:   (∀ a b, p ≤ a → a ≤ b → R a b)
182: 
183: /-- The peak is unique -/
184: theorem single_peaked_peak_unique {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p q : σ}
185:     (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hsq : single_peaked R q) (hne : p ≠ q) : False := by
186:   have hPpq : P R p q := hsp.1 q hne.symm
187:   have hPqp : P R q p := hsq.1 p hne
188:   exact hPpq.2 hPqp.1
189: 
190: /-- Single-peaked implies the peak is the best element (under Reflexive R) -/
191: theorem single_peaked_peak_best {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p : σ} {S : Finset σ}
192:     (hrefl : Reflexive R) (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hpS : p ∈ S) :
193:     is_best_element p S R := by
194:   intro y hy
195:   by_cases heq : y = p
196:   · subst heq; exact hrefl y
197:   · exact (hsp.1 y heq).1
198: 
199: /-- A profile is single-peaked if every voter has single-peaked preferences -/
200: def single_peaked_profile (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ) : Prop :=
201:   ∀ i : ι, single_peaked (prof i).rel (peaks i)
202: 
203: /-! ## Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)
204: 
205: For an odd number of voters with single-peaked preferences over a linearly
206: ordered set, the median peak (the middle element of sorted peaks) is a
207: Condorcet winner under majority rule.
208: 
209: Proof sketch:
210: - For any y < median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak >= median,
211:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
212: - For any y > median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak <= median,
213:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
214: -/

216: /-- Sorted list of peaks (with duplicates preserved) -/
217: noncomputable def sorted_peaks_list (peaks : ι → σ) : List σ :=
218:   (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).mergeSort (· ≤ ·)
219: 
220: /-- The median peak: the middle element of sorted peaks.
221:     For odd n, this is the unique middle element. -/
222: noncomputable def median_peak [Inhabited σ] (peaks : ι → σ) : σ :=
223:   let s := sorted_peaks_list peaks
224:   s.getD (s.length / 2) default

attributes.gen_ai.system_instructions:
[{"type": "text", "content": "Genere des tactiques Lean 4 et soumet-les au verificateur.

OUTILS PRINCIPAUX:
1. get_proof_state() → but courant + lemmes du SearchAgent + plan du Coordinator
2. file_read_lines(start, end) → lit le contexte autour du sorry (declaration, hyps)
3. compile_probe_goal(line) → SI tu as besoin de re-extraire le but (rare)
4. generate_tactics(goal) → suggestions tactiques heuristiques
5. submit_tactic(tactic) → tente UNE tactique complete (fait l'edit + lake build)
6. submit_decomposition(name, goal, sub_proof, main_proof) → introduit `have`
7. compile() → check apres edit (build SUCCESS + sorry delta + axioms)

STRATEGIE D'ATTAQUE (du simple au structurel):
1. Une-shot: rfl, decide, omega, simp, exact <lemme>
2. Adaptation: simp [<lemme>], exact <lemme> _, rw [<lemme>]
3. Combinaison: constructor + sub-tactiques, refine ⟨_, _⟩, cases/induction
4. Decomposition: si la preuve excede 4-5 lignes ou si le but est complexe,
   utilise submit_decomposition pour creer un `have h : sub := by ...` et
   reduire le but principal. PROGRES STRUCTUREL = sorry qui se rapproche
   d'un sous-but plus simple, meme si le compteur ne baisse pas immediatement.

QUAND UTILISER submit_decomposition:
- Conjonctions, equivalences, quantificateurs imbriques
- Buts arithmetiques avec sous-bornes a etablir
- Counting / cardinalite necessitant transfer (Finset → List → countP)
- Quand 2 essais one-shot ont echoue : DECOMPOSE plutot que d'iterer

CYCLE D'ITERATION (si echec):
- Lire l'erreur compilateur (elle est explicite)
- Adapter (ajouter named arg, changer un lemme, ajouter un cast)
- Si 2 adaptations identiques echouent : DECOMPOSER ou demander plus de lemmes
- Si 3 echecs cumules sur le meme sorry : laisser le Critic / Coordinator reformuler

INTERDIT (verifie toujours dans FAILED ATTEMPTS):
- Repeter une tactique deja en echec (meme texte exact)
- Utiliser sorry sur du code metier existant (anti-regression)
- Modifier le theoreme cible ou ses hypotheses pour faire passer la preuve

FIX PATTERNS GENERAUX (heuristiques, pas hard-codes):
- "type mismatch" → caster (norm_cast / push_cast / Nat.cast_*) ou ajouter `show`
- "unsolved goals" → la tactique laisse des sous-buts → enchainer ou decomposer
- "unfold failed" → la def est opaque → utiliser show ou unfold explicite
- "function expected" → arg implicite manquant, ajouter (a := ...) (b := ...)
- "omega failed" → l'expression n'est pas Nat/Int pure → caster d'abord

IMPORTANT — TON OBJECTIF:
Tu construis une preuve INCREMENTALEMENT. Chaque sous-but qui compile est un
gain meme si le sorry global subsiste. Ne te bloque pas en cherchant le coup
parfait : essaie, lis l'erreur, adapte. Le harnais te donne le retour en
quelques secondes."}]

2:   Social Choice Theory - Voting Definitions
3:   ==========================================
4: 
5:   Port of chasenorman/Formalized-Voting to Lean 4
6:   Original: https://github.com/chasenorman/Formalized-Voting
7: 
8:   Adapted to our SocialChoice/Basic.lean framework:
9:   - Uses PrefOrder from Basic.lean instead of raw relations
10:   - Uses P (strict preference) and I (indifference) from Basic.lean
11:   - Compatible with Profile ι σ from Arrow.lean
12: 
13:   Key concepts:
14:   - Margin function for pairwise vote counting
15:   - Condorcet winner/loser and their criteria
16:   - Pareto efficiency and monotonicity for Social Choice Correspondences
17: -/

19: import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Card
20: import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Basic
21: import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Basic
22: import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Sort
23: import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Powerset
24: import Mathlib.Order.Defs.LinearOrder

27: import SocialChoice.Basic

29: namespace SocialChoice

31: variable {ι σ : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ]
32: 
33: /-! ## Margin Function -/
34: 
35: /-- The margin of x over y in a profile:
36:     |{voters strictly preferring x to y}| - |{voters strictly preferring y to x}|
37:     Positive margin means x beats y in pairwise comparison. -/
38: noncomputable def margin (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : ℤ :=
39:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x y) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
40:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel y x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
41:   ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x y) Finset.univ).card : ℤ) -
42:     ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel y x) Finset.univ).card : ℤ)
43: 
44: /-- Positive margin: x beats y in pairwise comparison -/
45: def margin_pos (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : Prop :=
46:   0 < margin prof x y
47: 
48: /-! ## Condorcet Concepts -/
49: 
50: /-- A Condorcet winner: strictly beats every other alternative in pairwise comparison -/
51: def condorcet_winner (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
52:   x ∈ S ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof x y
53: 
54: /-- A Condorcet loser: strictly loses to every other alternative -/
55: def condorcet_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
56:   x ∈ S ∧ S.card ≥ 2 ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof y x
57: 
58: /-! ## Social Choice Correspondence -/
59: 
60: /-- A Social Choice Correspondence: maps profiles and alternative sets to chosen alternatives -/
61: def SCC (ι σ : Type*) [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ] :=
62:   (ι → PrefOrder σ) → Finset σ → Finset σ
63: 
64: /-! ## Voting Criteria -/
65: 
66: /-- Condorcet criterion: every Condorcet winner is selected -/
67: def condorcet_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
68:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_winner prof S x → x ∈ f prof S
69: 
70: /-- Condorcet loser criterion: no Condorcet loser is selected -/
71: def condorcet_loser_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
72:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_loser prof S x → x ∉ f prof S
73: 
74: /-- Pareto efficiency: if all voters prefer x to y, then y is not selected -/
75: def pareto_scc (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
76:   ∀ prof S x y, x ∈ S → y ∈ S →
77:     (∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) → y ∉ f prof S
78: 
79: /-- Monotonicity: improving x's position preserves x's selection -/
80: def monotonicity (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
81:   ∀ prof prof' S x,
82:     x ∈ f prof S → x ∈ S →
83:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, P (prof i).rel x y → P (prof' i).rel x y) →
84:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, ¬P (prof i).rel y x → ¬P (prof' i).rel y x) →
85:     x ∈ f prof' S
86: 
87: /-! ## Margin Properties -/
88: 
89: theorem margin_antisymm (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) :
90:     margin prof x y = - (margin prof y x) := by
91:   unfold margin
92:   ring
93: 
94: theorem margin_self (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x : σ) :
95:     margin prof x x = 0 := by
96:   unfold margin
97:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
98:   have hN : ∀ i : ι, ¬P (prof i).rel x x := fun _ => false_of_P_self
99:   have h_empty : Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x x) Finset.univ = ∅ := by
100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi"}]}, {"role": "assistant", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "name": "file_read_lines", "arguments": "{"start":100,"end":250}"}]}, {"role": "tool", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call_response", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "response": "100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi
101:   simp
102: 
103: theorem margin_pos_iff_neg_rev (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ} :
104:     margin_pos prof x y ↔ margin prof y x < 0 := by
105:   unfold margin_pos
106:   rw [margin_antisymm prof]
107:   omega
108: 
109: /-! ## Condorcet Properties -/
110: 
111: /-- The Condorcet winner is unique (when it exists) -/
112: theorem condorcet_winner_unique (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x y : σ}
113:     (hx : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hy : condorcet_winner prof S y) (hxy : x ≠ y) :
114:     False := by
115:   obtain ⟨hxs, hbeat⟩ := hx
116:   obtain ⟨hys, hbeat'⟩ := hy
117:   have h₁ := hbeat y hys hxy.symm
118:   have h₂ := hbeat' x hxs hxy
119:   unfold margin_pos at h₁ h₂
120:   have := margin_antisymm prof x y
121:   linarith
122: 
123: /-- A Condorcet winner cannot be a Condorcet loser -/
124: theorem condorcet_winner_not_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x : σ}
125:     (hw : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hl : condorcet_loser prof S x) :
126:     False := by
127:   obtain ⟨hxs, hcard, hlose⟩ := hl
128:   obtain ⟨_, hbeat⟩ := hw
129:   obtain ⟨y, hyS, hxy⟩ := exists_second_distinct_mem (by omega : 2 ≤ S.card) hxs
130:   have h₁ := hbeat y hyS hxy
131:   have h₂ := hlose y hyS hxy
132:   unfold margin_pos at h₁ h₂
133:   have := margin_antisymm prof x y
134:   linarith
135: 
136: /-! ## Unanimity and Margin -/
137: 
138: /-- If all voters strictly prefer x to y, the margin is positive -/
139: theorem margin_pos_of_unanimous [Nonempty ι] (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ}
140:     (h : ∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) : margin_pos prof x y := by
141:   unfold margin_pos
142:   -- Compute margin value: all voters prefer x to y, so margin = card ι > 0
143:   have hkey : margin prof x y = (Fintype.card ι : ℤ) := by
144:     unfold margin
145:     classical
146:     -- After classical, Decidable instances are uniform via Classical.dec
147:     -- The haveI in margin's definition and our context both resolve to Classical.dec
148:     have hNyx : ∀ i, ¬P (prof i).rel y x := fun i hi => (h i).2 hi.1
149:     have h_univ : (Finset.univ : Finset ι).filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x y) = Finset.univ := by
150:       ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact h i
151:     have h_empty : (Finset.univ : Finset ι).filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel y x) = ∅ := by
152:       ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hNyx i hi
153:     simp [h_univ, h_empty, Finset.card_univ]
154:   rw [hkey]
155:   exact mod_cast (Finset.card_pos.mpr Finset.univ_nonempty)
156: 
157: /-! ## Single-Peaked Preferences
158: 
159: Reference: Black (1948), "On the Rationale of Group Decision-making"
160: 
161: A preference ordering over a linearly ordered set is single-peaked if there
162: exists a unique most-preferred alternative (the peak) and preferences decline
163: monotonically away from the peak in both directions.
164: -/
165: 
166: section SinglePeaked
167: 
168: variable [LinearOrder σ]
169: 
170: /-- The peak of a preference relation: the unique strictly most-preferred element -/
171: def is_peak (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
172:   ∀ x, x ≠ p → P R p x
173: 
174: /-- R is single-peaked with peak p on a linearly ordered set.
175:     1. p is the peak (strictly preferred to everything else)
176:     2. Left of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (a ≤ b ≤ p → R b a)
177:     3. Right of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (p ≤ a ≤ b → R a b) -/
178: def single_peaked (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
179:   is_peak R p ∧
180:   (∀ a b, a ≤ b → b ≤ p → R b a) ∧
181:   (∀ a b, p ≤ a → a ≤ b → R a b)
182: 
183: /-- The peak is unique -/
184: theorem single_peaked_peak_unique {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p q : σ}
185:     (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hsq : single_peaked R q) (hne : p ≠ q) : False := by
186:   have hPpq : P R p q := hsp.1 q hne.symm
187:   have hPqp : P R q p := hsq.1 p hne
188:   exact hPpq.2 hPqp.1
189: 
190: /-- Single-peaked implies the peak is the best element (under Reflexive R) -/
191: theorem single_peaked_peak_best {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p : σ} {S : Finset σ}
192:     (hrefl : Reflexive R) (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hpS : p ∈ S) :
193:     is_best_element p S R := by
194:   intro y hy
195:   by_cases heq : y = p
196:   · subst heq; exact hrefl y
197:   · exact (hsp.1 y heq).1
198: 
199: /-- A profile is single-peaked if every voter has single-peaked preferences -/
200: def single_peaked_profile (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ) : Prop :=
201:   ∀ i : ι, single_peaked (prof i).rel (peaks i)
202: 
203: /-! ## Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)
204: 
205: For an odd number of voters with single-peaked preferences over a linearly
206: ordered set, the median peak (the middle element of sorted peaks) is a
207: Condorcet winner under majority rule.
208: 
209: Proof sketch:
210: - For any y < median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak >= median,
211:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
212: - For any y > median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak <= median,
213:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
214: -/
215: 
216: /-- Sorted list of peaks (with duplicates preserved) -/
217: noncomputable def sorted_peaks_list (peaks : ι → σ) : List σ :=
218:   (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).mergeSort (· ≤ ·)
219: 
220: /-- The median peak: the middle element of sorted peaks.
221:     For odd n, this is the unique middle element. -/
222: noncomputable def median_peak [Inhabited σ] (peaks : ι → σ) : σ :=
223:   let s := sorted_peaks_list peaks
224:   s.getD (s.length / 2) default
225: 
226: /-- **Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)**: For an odd number of voters with
227:     single-peaked preferences, the median peak is a Condorcet winner. -/
228: theorem median_voter_theorem (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ)
229:     (hsp : single_peaked_profile prof peaks)
230:     (hodd : Odd (Fintype.card ι)) :
231:     ∃ m, condorcet_winner prof (Finset.univ.image peaks) m := by

attributes.gen_ai.system_instructions:
[{"type": "text", "content": "Genere des tactiques Lean 4 et soumet-les au verificateur.

OUTILS PRINCIPAUX:
1. get_proof_state() → but courant + lemmes du SearchAgent + plan du Coordinator
2. file_read_lines(start, end) → lit le contexte autour du sorry (declaration, hyps)
3. compile_probe_goal(line) → SI tu as besoin de re-extraire le but (rare)
4. generate_tactics(goal) → suggestions tactiques heuristiques
5. submit_tactic(tactic) → tente UNE tactique complete (fait l'edit + lake build)
6. submit_decomposition(name, goal, sub_proof, main_proof) → introduit `have`
7. compile() → check apres edit (build SUCCESS + sorry delta + axioms)

STRATEGIE D'ATTAQUE (du simple au structurel):
1. Une-shot: rfl, decide, omega, simp, exact <lemme>
2. Adaptation: simp [<lemme>], exact <lemme> _, rw [<lemme>]
3. Combinaison: constructor + sub-tactiques, refine ⟨_, _⟩, cases/induction
4. Decomposition: si la preuve excede 4-5 lignes ou si le but est complexe,
   utilise submit_decomposition pour creer un `have h : sub := by ...` et
   reduire le but principal. PROGRES STRUCTUREL = sorry qui se rapproche
   d'un sous-but plus simple, meme si le compteur ne baisse pas immediatement.

QUAND UTILISER submit_decomposition:
- Conjonctions, equivalences, quantificateurs imbriques
- Buts arithmetiques avec sous-bornes a etablir
- Counting / cardinalite necessitant transfer (Finset → List → countP)
- Quand 2 essais one-shot ont echoue : DECOMPOSE plutot que d'iterer

CYCLE D'ITERATION (si echec):
- Lire l'erreur compilateur (elle est explicite)
- Adapter (ajouter named arg, changer un lemme, ajouter un cast)
- Si 2 adaptations identiques echouent : DECOMPOSER ou demander plus de lemmes
- Si 3 echecs cumules sur le meme sorry : laisser le Critic / Coordinator reformuler

INTERDIT (verifie toujours dans FAILED ATTEMPTS):
- Repeter une tactique deja en echec (meme texte exact)
- Utiliser sorry sur du code metier existant (anti-regression)
- Modifier le theoreme cible ou ses hypotheses pour faire passer la preuve

FIX PATTERNS GENERAUX (heuristiques, pas hard-codes):
- "type mismatch" → caster (norm_cast / push_cast / Nat.cast_*) ou ajouter `show`
- "unsolved goals" → la tactique laisse des sous-buts → enchainer ou decomposer
- "unfold failed" → la def est opaque → utiliser show ou unfold explicite
- "function expected" → arg implicite manquant, ajouter (a := ...) (b := ...)
- "omega failed" → l'expression n'est pas Nat/Int pure → caster d'abord

IMPORTANT — TON OBJECTIF:
Tu construis une preuve INCREMENTALEMENT. Chaque sous-but qui compile est un
gain meme si le sorry global subsiste. Ne te bloque pas en cherchant le coup
parfait : essaie, lis l'erreur, adapte. Le harnais te donne le retour en
quelques secondes."}]

attributes.gen_ai.tool.description:
Replace the sorry at a given line with a tactic. Auto-detects indentation.

        When `build_check=True` (default), runs lake build after writing and
        reverts the file if compilation fails. The autonomous prover loop also
        does periodic build checks, but inline validation here catches stubs
        like `exact _` immediately and gives the agent a fresh diagnostic.
attributes.gen_ai.tool.call.arguments:
{"sorry_line": 325, "replacement": "    unfold median_peak sorted_peaks_list
    set l := (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).mergeSort (· ≤ ·) with hdef
    have hl : l.length = Fintype.card ι := by
      simp [hdef, List.length_mergeSort, List.length_map, Finset.length_toList]
    have hperm : l ≈ Finset.univ.toList.map peaks := List.mergeSort_perm _ _
    -- median is at index n/2 in sorted list
    have hmed_get : l.get? (l.length / 2) = some (median_peak peaks) := by
      simp [median_peak, sorted_peaks_list, hdef, List.get?_eq_getElem]
      have hn : l.length / 2 < l.length := by omega
      simp [List.getD, List.getElem?_eq_getElem, hn]
    sorry", "build_check": true}

31: variable {ι σ : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ]
32: 
33: /-! ## Margin Function -/
34: 
35: /-- The margin of x over y in a profile:
36:     |{voters strictly preferring x to y}| - |{voters strictly preferring y to x}|
37:     Positive margin means x beats y in pairwise comparison. -/
38: noncomputable def margin (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : ℤ :=
39:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x y) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
40:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel y x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
41:   ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x y) Finset.univ).card : ℤ) -
42:     ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel y x) Finset.univ).card : ℤ)
43: 
44: /-- Positive margin: x beats y in pairwise comparison -/
45: def margin_pos (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : Prop :=
46:   0 < margin prof x y
47: 
48: /-! ## Condorcet Concepts -/
49: 
50: /-- A Condorcet winner: strictly beats every other alternative in pairwise comparison -/
51: def condorcet_winner (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
52:   x ∈ S ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof x y
53: 
54: /-- A Condorcet loser: strictly loses to every other alternative -/
55: def condorcet_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
56:   x ∈ S ∧ S.card ≥ 2 ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof y x
57: 
58: /-! ## Social Choice Correspondence -/
59: 
60: /-- A Social Choice Correspondence: maps profiles and alternative sets to chosen alternatives -/
61: def SCC (ι σ : Type*) [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ] :=
62:   (ι → PrefOrder σ) → Finset σ → Finset σ
63: 
64: /-! ## Voting Criteria -/
65: 
66: /-- Condorcet criterion: every Condorcet winner is selected -/
67: def condorcet_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
68:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_winner prof S x → x ∈ f prof S
69: 
70: /-- Condorcet loser criterion: no Condorcet loser is selected -/
71: def condorcet_loser_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
72:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_loser prof S x → x ∉ f prof S
73: 
74: /-- Pareto efficiency: if all voters prefer x to y, then y is not selected -/
75: def pareto_scc (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
76:   ∀ prof S x y, x ∈ S → y ∈ S →
77:     (∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) → y ∉ f prof S
78: 
79: /-- Monotonicity: improving x's position preserves x's selection -/
80: def monotonicity (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
81:   ∀ prof prof' S x,
82:     x ∈ f prof S → x ∈ S →
83:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, P (prof i).rel x y → P (prof' i).rel x y) →
84:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, ¬P (prof i).rel y x → ¬P (prof' i).rel y x) →
85:     x ∈ f prof' S
86: 
87: /-! ## Margin Properties -/
88: 
89: theorem margin_antisymm (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) :
90:     margin prof x y = - (margin prof y x) := by
91:   unfold margin
92:   ring
93: 
94: theorem margin_self (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x : σ) :
95:     margin prof x x = 0 := by
96:   unfold margin
97:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
98:   have hN : ∀ i : ι, ¬P (prof i).rel x x := fun _ => false_of_P_self
99:   have h_empty : Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x x) Finset.univ = ∅ := by
100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi"}]}, {"role": "assistant", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "name": "file_read_lines", "arguments": "{"start":100,"end":250}"}]}, {"role": "tool", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call_response", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "response": "100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi

103: theorem margin_pos_iff_neg_rev (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ} :
104:     margin_pos prof x y ↔ margin prof y x < 0 := by

106:   rw [margin_antisymm prof]

109: /-! ## Condorcet Properties -/
110: 
111: /-- The Condorcet winner is unique (when it exists) -/
112: theorem condorcet_winner_unique (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x y : σ}
113:     (hx : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hy : condorcet_winner prof S y) (hxy : x ≠ y) :

117:   have h₁ := hbeat y hys hxy.symm
118:   have h₂ := hbeat' x hxs hxy
119:   unfold margin_pos at h₁ h₂

121:   linarith

123: /-- A Condorcet winner cannot be a Condorcet loser -/
124: theorem condorcet_winner_not_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x : σ}
125:     (hw : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hl : condorcet_loser prof S x) :

157: /-! ## Single-Peaked Preferences
158: 
159: Reference: Black (1948), "On the Rationale of Group Decision-making"
160: 
161: A preference ordering over a linearly ordered set is single-peaked if there
162: exists a unique most-preferred alternative (the peak) and preferences decline
163: monotonically away from the peak in both directions.
164: -/
165: 
166: section SinglePeaked
167: 
168: variable [LinearOrder σ]
169: 
170: /-- The peak of a preference relation: the unique strictly most-preferred element -/
171: def is_peak (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
172:   ∀ x, x ≠ p → P R p x
173: 
174: /-- R is single-peaked with peak p on a linearly ordered set.
175:     1. p is the peak (strictly preferred to everything else)
176:     2. Left of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (a ≤ b ≤ p → R b a)
177:     3. Right of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (p ≤ a ≤ b → R a b) -/
178: def single_peaked (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
179:   is_peak R p ∧
180:   (∀ a b, a ≤ b → b ≤ p → R b a) ∧
181:   (∀ a b, p ≤ a → a ≤ b → R a b)
182: 
183: /-- The peak is unique -/
184: theorem single_peaked_peak_unique {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p q : σ}
185:     (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hsq : single_peaked R q) (hne : p ≠ q) : False := by
186:   have hPpq : P R p q := hsp.1 q hne.symm
187:   have hPqp : P R q p := hsq.1 p hne
188:   exact hPpq.2 hPqp.1
189: 
190: /-- Single-peaked implies the peak is the best element (under Reflexive R) -/
191: theorem single_peaked_peak_best {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p : σ} {S : Finset σ}
192:     (hrefl : Reflexive R) (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hpS : p ∈ S) :
193:     is_best_element p S R := by
194:   intro y hy
195:   by_cases heq : y = p
196:   · subst heq; exact hrefl y
197:   · exact (hsp.1 y heq).1
198: 
199: /-- A profile is single-peaked if every voter has single-peaked preferences -/
200: def single_peaked_profile (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ) : Prop :=
201:   ∀ i : ι, single_peaked (prof i).rel (peaks i)
202: 
203: /-! ## Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)
204: 
205: For an odd number of voters with single-peaked preferences over a linearly
206: ordered set, the median peak (the middle element of sorted peaks) is a
207: Condorcet winner under majority rule.
208: 
209: Proof sketch:
210: - For any y < median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak >= median,
211:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
212: - For any y > median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak <= median,
213:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
214: -/
215: 
216: /-- Sorted list of peaks (with duplicates preserved) -/
217: noncomputable def sorted_peaks_list (peaks : ι → σ) : List σ :=
218:   (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).mergeSort (· ≤ ·)
219: 
220: /-- The median peak: the middle element of sorted peaks.
221:     For odd n, this is the unique middle element. -/
222: noncomputable def median_peak [Inhabited σ] (peaks : ι → σ) : σ :=
223:   let s := sorted_peaks_list peaks
224:   s.getD (s.length / 2) default
225: 
226: /-- **Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)**: For an odd number of voters with
227:     single-peaked preferences, the median peak is a Condorcet winner. -/
228: theorem median_voter_theorem (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ)
229:     (hsp : single_peaked_profile prof peaks)
230:     (hodd : Odd (Fintype.card ι)) :

236:   haveI : Inhabited σ := Classical.inhabited_of_nonempty (hne.map peaks)

attributes.gen_ai.system_instructions:
[{"type": "text", "content": "Genere des tactiques Lean 4 et soumet-les au verificateur.

OUTILS PRINCIPAUX:
1. get_proof_state() → but courant + lemmes du SearchAgent + plan du Coordinator
2. file_read_lines(start, end) → lit le contexte autour du sorry (declaration, hyps)
3. compile_probe_goal(line) → SI tu as besoin de re-extraire le but (rare)
4. generate_tactics(goal) → suggestions tactiques heuristiques
5. submit_tactic(tactic) → tente UNE tactique complete (fait l'edit + lake build)
6. submit_decomposition(name, goal, sub_proof, main_proof) → introduit `have`
7. compile() → check apres edit (build SUCCESS + sorry delta + axioms)

STRATEGIE D'ATTAQUE (du simple au structurel):
1. Une-shot: rfl, decide, omega, simp, exact <lemme>
2. Adaptation: simp [<lemme>], exact <lemme> _, rw [<lemme>]
3. Combinaison: constructor + sub-tactiques, refine ⟨_, _⟩, cases/induction
4. Decomposition: si la preuve excede 4-5 lignes ou si le but est complexe,
   utilise submit_decomposition pour creer un `have h : sub := by ...` et
   reduire le but principal. PROGRES STRUCTUREL = sorry qui se rapproche
   d'un sous-but plus simple, meme si le compteur ne baisse pas immediatement.

QUAND UTILISER submit_decomposition:
- Conjonctions, equivalences, quantificateurs imbriques
- Buts arithmetiques avec sous-bornes a etablir
- Counting / cardinalite necessitant transfer (Finset → List → countP)
- Quand 2 essais one-shot ont echoue : DECOMPOSE plutot que d'iterer

CYCLE D'ITERATION (si echec):
- Lire l'erreur compilateur (elle est explicite)
- Adapter (ajouter named arg, changer un lemme, ajouter un cast)
- Si 2 adaptations identiques echouent : DECOMPOSER ou demander plus de lemmes
- Si 3 echecs cumules sur le meme sorry : laisser le Critic / Coordinator reformuler

INTERDIT (verifie toujours dans FAILED ATTEMPTS):
- Repeter une tactique deja en echec (meme texte exact)
- Utiliser sorry sur du code metier existant (anti-regression)
- Modifier le theoreme cible ou ses hypotheses pour faire passer la preuve

FIX PATTERNS GENERAUX (heuristiques, pas hard-codes):
- "type mismatch" → caster (norm_cast / push_cast / Nat.cast_*) ou ajouter `show`
- "unsolved goals" → la tactique laisse des sous-buts → enchainer ou decomposer
- "unfold failed" → la def est opaque → utiliser show ou unfold explicite
- "function expected" → arg implicite manquant, ajouter (a := ...) (b := ...)
- "omega failed" → l'expression n'est pas Nat/Int pure → caster d'abord

IMPORTANT — TON OBJECTIF:
Tu construis une preuve INCREMENTALEMENT. Chaque sous-but qui compile est un
gain meme si le sorry global subsiste. Ne te bloque pas en cherchant le coup
parfait : essaie, lis l'erreur, adapte. Le harnais te donne le retour en
quelques secondes."}]

- There are at least `k+1` elements ≥ the median
- So `|{i : peaks i < median}| ≤ k` and `|{i : median ≤ peaks i}| ≥ k+1`
- Hence `|{i : peaks i < median}| < |{i : median ≤ peaks i}|`

- `A.card ≤ n/2` (from sortedness)

But how do we show `|{i | peaks i < median}| ≤ n/2` without using the sorted list? We need the sorted list!

OK let me try to figure out the Lean 4 API for mergeSort sortedness. attributes.gen_ai.tool.description:
Replace the sorry at a given line with a tactic. Auto-detects indentation.

        When `build_check=True` (default), runs lake build after writing and
        reverts the file if compilation fails. The autonomous prover loop also
        does periodic build checks, but inline validation here catches stubs
        like `exact _` immediately and gives the agent a fresh diagnostic.

    have hl : l.length = Fintype.card ι := by
      simp [hdef, List.length_mergeSort, List.length_map, Finset.length_toList]
    have hperm : l ≈ Finset.univ.toList.map peaks := List.mergeSort_perm _ _

    -- l is sorted: elements at index >= n/2 are >= l[n/2] = median

    sorry", "build_check": true}

31: variable {ι σ : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ]
32: 
33: /-! ## Margin Function -/
34: 
35: /-- The margin of x over y in a profile:
36:     |{voters strictly preferring x to y}| - |{voters strictly preferring y to x}|
37:     Positive margin means x beats y in pairwise comparison. -/
38: noncomputable def margin (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : ℤ :=
39:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x y) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
40:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel y x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
41:   ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x y) Finset.univ).card : ℤ) -
42:     ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel y x) Finset.univ).card : ℤ)
43: 
44: /-- Positive margin: x beats y in pairwise comparison -/
45: def margin_pos (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : Prop :=
46:   0 < margin prof x y
47: 
48: /-! ## Condorcet Concepts -/
49: 
50: /-- A Condorcet winner: strictly beats every other alternative in pairwise comparison -/
51: def condorcet_winner (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
52:   x ∈ S ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof x y
53: 
54: /-- A Condorcet loser: strictly loses to every other alternative -/
55: def condorcet_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
56:   x ∈ S ∧ S.card ≥ 2 ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof y x
57: 
58: /-! ## Social Choice Correspondence -/
59: 
60: /-- A Social Choice Correspondence: maps profiles and alternative sets to chosen alternatives -/
61: def SCC (ι σ : Type*) [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ] :=
62:   (ι → PrefOrder σ) → Finset σ → Finset σ
63: 
64: /-! ## Voting Criteria -/
65: 
66: /-- Condorcet criterion: every Condorcet winner is selected -/
67: def condorcet_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
68:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_winner prof S x → x ∈ f prof S
69: 
70: /-- Condorcet loser criterion: no Condorcet loser is selected -/
71: def condorcet_loser_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
72:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_loser prof S x → x ∉ f prof S
73: 
74: /-- Pareto efficiency: if all voters prefer x to y, then y is not selected -/
75: def pareto_scc (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
76:   ∀ prof S x y, x ∈ S → y ∈ S →
77:     (∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) → y ∉ f prof S
78: 
79: /-- Monotonicity: improving x's position preserves x's selection -/
80: def monotonicity (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
81:   ∀ prof prof' S x,
82:     x ∈ f prof S → x ∈ S →
83:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, P (prof i).rel x y → P (prof' i).rel x y) →
84:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, ¬P (prof i).rel y x → ¬P (prof' i).rel y x) →
85:     x ∈ f prof' S
86: 
87: /-! ## Margin Properties -/

89: theorem margin_antisymm (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) :
90:     margin prof x y = - (margin prof y x) := by
91:   unfold margin
92:   ring
93: 
94: theorem margin_self (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x : σ) :
95:     margin prof x x = 0 := by
96:   unfold margin
97:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
98:   have hN : ∀ i : ι, ¬P (prof i).rel x x := fun _ => false_of_P_self
99:   have h_empty : Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x x) Finset.univ = ∅ := by
100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi"}]}, {"role": "assistant", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "name": "file_read_lines", "arguments": "{"start":100,"end":250}"}]}, {"role": "tool", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call_response", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "response": "100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi

103: theorem margin_pos_iff_neg_rev (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ} :

106:   rw [margin_antisymm prof]

109: /-! ## Condorcet Properties -/

111: /-- The Condorcet winner is unique (when it exists) -/
112: theorem condorcet_winner_unique (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x y : σ}
113:     (hx : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hy : condorcet_winner prof S y) (hxy : x ≠ y) :

116:   obtain ⟨hys, hbeat'⟩ := hy
117:   have h₁ := hbeat y hys hxy.symm
118:   have h₂ := hbeat' x hxs hxy
119:   unfold margin_pos at h₁ h₂
120:   have := margin_antisymm prof x y
121:   linarith

123: /-- A Condorcet winner cannot be a Condorcet loser -/
124: theorem condorcet_winner_not_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x : σ}

157: /-! ## Single-Peaked Preferences
158: 
159: Reference: Black (1948), "On the Rationale of Group Decision-making"
160: 
161: A preference ordering over a linearly ordered set is single-peaked if there
162: exists a unique most-preferred alternative (the peak) and preferences decline
163: monotonically away from the peak in both directions.
164: -/
165: 
166: section SinglePeaked
167: 
168: variable [LinearOrder σ]
169: 
170: /-- The peak of a preference relation: the unique strictly most-preferred element -/
171: def is_peak (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
172:   ∀ x, x ≠ p → P R p x
173: 
174: /-- R is single-peaked with peak p on a linearly ordered set.
175:     1. p is the peak (strictly preferred to everything else)
176:     2. Left of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (a ≤ b ≤ p → R b a)
177:     3. Right of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (p ≤ a ≤ b → R a b) -/
178: def single_peaked (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
179:   is_peak R p ∧
180:   (∀ a b, a ≤ b → b ≤ p → R b a) ∧
181:   (∀ a b, p ≤ a → a ≤ b → R a b)
182: 
183: /-- The peak is unique -/
184: theorem single_peaked_peak_unique {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p q : σ}
185:     (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hsq : single_peaked R q) (hne : p ≠ q) : False := by
186:   have hPpq : P R p q := hsp.1 q hne.symm
187:   have hPqp : P R q p := hsq.1 p hne
188:   exact hPpq.2 hPqp.1
189: 
190: /-- Single-peaked implies the peak is the best element (under Reflexive R) -/
191: theorem single_peaked_peak_best {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p : σ} {S : Finset σ}
192:     (hrefl : Reflexive R) (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hpS : p ∈ S) :
193:     is_best_element p S R := by
194:   intro y hy
195:   by_cases heq : y = p
196:   · subst heq; exact hrefl y
197:   · exact (hsp.1 y heq).1
198: 
199: /-- A profile is single-peaked if every voter has single-peaked preferences -/
200: def single_peaked_profile (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ) : Prop :=
201:   ∀ i : ι, single_peaked (prof i).rel (peaks i)
202: 
203: /-! ## Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)
204: 
205: For an odd number of voters with single-peaked preferences over a linearly
206: ordered set, the median peak (the middle element of sorted peaks) is a
207: Condorcet winner under majority rule.
208: 
209: Proof sketch:
210: - For any y < median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak >= median,
211:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
212: - For any y > median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak <= median,
213:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
214: -/
215: 
216: /-- Sorted list of peaks (with duplicates preserved) -/
217: noncomputable def sorted_peaks_list (peaks : ι → σ) : List σ :=
218:   (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).mergeSort (· ≤ ·)
219: 
220: /-- The median peak: the middle element of sorted peaks.
221:     For odd n, this is the unique middle element. -/
222: noncomputable def median_peak [Inhabited σ] (peaks : ι → σ) : σ :=
223:   let s := sorted_peaks_list peaks
224:   s.getD (s.length / 2) default
225:

227:     single-peaked preferences, the median peak is a Condorcet winner. -/

attributes.gen_ai.system_instructions:
[{"type": "text", "content": "Genere des tactiques Lean 4 et soumet-les au verificateur.

OUTILS PRINCIPAUX:
1. get_proof_state() → but courant + lemmes du SearchAgent + plan du Coordinator
2. file_read_lines(start, end) → lit le contexte autour du sorry (declaration, hyps)
3. compile_probe_goal(line) → SI tu as besoin de re-extraire le but (rare)
4. generate_tactics(goal) → suggestions tactiques heuristiques
5. submit_tactic(tactic) → tente UNE tactique complete (fait l'edit + lake build)
6. submit_decomposition(name, goal, sub_proof, main_proof) → introduit `have`
7. compile() → check apres edit (build SUCCESS + sorry delta + axioms)

STRATEGIE D'ATTAQUE (du simple au structurel):
1. Une-shot: rfl, decide, omega, simp, exact <lemme>
2. Adaptation: simp [<lemme>], exact <lemme> _, rw [<lemme>]
3. Combinaison: constructor + sub-tactiques, refine ⟨_, _⟩, cases/induction
4. Decomposition: si la preuve excede 4-5 lignes ou si le but est complexe,
   utilise submit_decomposition pour creer un `have h : sub := by ...` et
   reduire le but principal. PROGRES STRUCTUREL = sorry qui se rapproche
   d'un sous-but plus simple, meme si le compteur ne baisse pas immediatement.

QUAND UTILISER submit_decomposition:
- Conjonctions, equivalences, quantificateurs imbriques
- Buts arithmetiques avec sous-bornes a etablir
- Counting / cardinalite necessitant transfer (Finset → List → countP)
- Quand 2 essais one-shot ont echoue : DECOMPOSE plutot que d'iterer

CYCLE D'ITERATION (si echec):
- Lire l'erreur compilateur (elle est explicite)
- Adapter (ajouter named arg, changer un lemme, ajouter un cast)
- Si 2 adaptations identiques echouent : DECOMPOSER ou demander plus de lemmes
- Si 3 echecs cumules sur le meme sorry : laisser le Critic / Coordinator reformuler

INTERDIT (verifie toujours dans FAILED ATTEMPTS):
- Repeter une tactique deja en echec (meme texte exact)
- Utiliser sorry sur du code metier existant (anti-regression)
- Modifier le theoreme cible ou ses hypotheses pour faire passer la preuve

FIX PATTERNS GENERAUX (heuristiques, pas hard-codes):
- "type mismatch" → caster (norm_cast / push_cast / Nat.cast_*) ou ajouter `show`
- "unsolved goals" → la tactique laisse des sous-buts → enchainer ou decomposer
- "unfold failed" → la def est opaque → utiliser show ou unfold explicite
- "function expected" → arg implicite manquant, ajouter (a := ...) (b := ...)
- "omega failed" → l'expression n'est pas Nat/Int pure → caster d'abord

IMPORTANT — TON OBJECTIF:
Tu construis une preuve INCREMENTALEMENT. Chaque sous-but qui compile est un
gain meme si le sorry global subsiste. Ne te bloque pas en cherchant le coup
parfait : essaie, lis l'erreur, adapte. Le harnais te donne le retour en
quelques secondes."}]

31: variable {ι σ : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ]
32: 
33: /-! ## Margin Function -/
34: 
35: /-- The margin of x over y in a profile:
36:     |{voters strictly preferring x to y}| - |{voters strictly preferring y to x}|
37:     Positive margin means x beats y in pairwise comparison. -/
38: noncomputable def margin (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : ℤ :=
39:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x y) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
40:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel y x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
41:   ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x y) Finset.univ).card : ℤ) -
42:     ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel y x) Finset.univ).card : ℤ)
43: 
44: /-- Positive margin: x beats y in pairwise comparison -/
45: def margin_pos (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : Prop :=
46:   0 < margin prof x y
47: 
48: /-! ## Condorcet Concepts -/
49: 
50: /-- A Condorcet winner: strictly beats every other alternative in pairwise comparison -/
51: def condorcet_winner (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
52:   x ∈ S ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof x y
53: 
54: /-- A Condorcet loser: strictly loses to every other alternative -/
55: def condorcet_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
56:   x ∈ S ∧ S.card ≥ 2 ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof y x
57: 
58: /-! ## Social Choice Correspondence -/
59: 
60: /-- A Social Choice Correspondence: maps profiles and alternative sets to chosen alternatives -/
61: def SCC (ι σ : Type*) [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ] :=
62:   (ι → PrefOrder σ) → Finset σ → Finset σ
63: 
64: /-! ## Voting Criteria -/
65: 
66: /-- Condorcet criterion: every Condorcet winner is selected -/
67: def condorcet_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
68:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_winner prof S x → x ∈ f prof S
69: 
70: /-- Condorcet loser criterion: no Condorcet loser is selected -/
71: def condorcet_loser_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
72:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_loser prof S x → x ∉ f prof S
73: 
74: /-- Pareto efficiency: if all voters prefer x to y, then y is not selected -/
75: def pareto_scc (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
76:   ∀ prof S x y, x ∈ S → y ∈ S →
77:     (∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) → y ∉ f prof S
78: 
79: /-- Monotonicity: improving x's position preserves x's selection -/
80: def monotonicity (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
81:   ∀ prof prof' S x,
82:     x ∈ f prof S → x ∈ S →
83:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, P (prof i).rel x y → P (prof' i).rel x y) →
84:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, ¬P (prof i).rel y x → ¬P (prof' i).rel y x) →
85:     x ∈ f prof' S
86: 
87: /-! ## Margin Properties -/

89: theorem margin_antisymm (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) :
90:     margin prof x y = - (margin prof y x) := by
91:   unfold margin
92:   ring
93: 
94: theorem margin_self (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x : σ) :
95:     margin prof x x = 0 := by
96:   unfold margin
97:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
98:   have hN : ∀ i : ι, ¬P (prof i).rel x x := fun _ => false_of_P_self
99:   have h_empty : Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x x) Finset.univ = ∅ := by
100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi"}]}, {"role": "assistant", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "name": "file_read_lines", "arguments": "{"start":100,"end":250}"}]}, {"role": "tool", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call_response", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "response": "100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi

103: theorem margin_pos_iff_neg_rev (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ} :
104:     margin_pos prof x y ↔ margin prof y x < 0 := by

106:   rw [margin_antisymm prof]

109: /-! ## Condorcet Properties -/
110: 
111: /-- The Condorcet winner is unique (when it exists) -/
112: theorem condorcet_winner_unique (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x y : σ}
113:     (hx : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hy : condorcet_winner prof S y) (hxy : x ≠ y) :
114:     False := by
115:   obtain ⟨hxs, hbeat⟩ := hx
116:   obtain ⟨hys, hbeat'⟩ := hy
117:   have h₁ := hbeat y hys hxy.symm
118:   have h₂ := hbeat' x hxs hxy
119:   unfold margin_pos at h₁ h₂
120:   have := margin_antisymm prof x y
121:   linarith
122: 
123: /-- A Condorcet winner cannot be a Condorcet loser -/
124: theorem condorcet_winner_not_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x : σ}
125:     (hw : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hl : condorcet_loser prof S x) :
126:     False := by

128:   obtain ⟨_, hbeat⟩ := hw

130:   have h₁ := hbeat y hyS hxy
131:   have h₂ := hlose y hyS hxy

138: /-- If all voters strictly prefer x to y, the margin is positive -/
139: theorem margin_pos_of_unanimous [Nonempty ι] (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ}

157: /-! ## Single-Peaked Preferences
158: 
159: Reference: Black (1948), "On the Rationale of Group Decision-making"
160: 
161: A preference ordering over a linearly ordered set is single-peaked if there
162: exists a unique most-preferred alternative (the peak) and preferences decline
163: monotonically away from the peak in both directions.
164: -/
165: 
166: section SinglePeaked
167: 
168: variable [LinearOrder σ]
169: 
170: /-- The peak of a preference relation: the unique strictly most-preferred element -/
171: def is_peak (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
172:   ∀ x, x ≠ p → P R p x
173: 
174: /-- R is single-peaked with peak p on a linearly ordered set.
175:     1. p is the peak (strictly preferred to everything else)
176:     2. Left of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (a ≤ b ≤ p → R b a)
177:     3. Right of peak: closer to peak is weakly preferred (p ≤ a ≤ b → R a b) -/
178: def single_peaked (R : σ → σ → Prop) (p : σ) : Prop :=
179:   is_peak R p ∧
180:   (∀ a b, a ≤ b → b ≤ p → R b a) ∧
181:   (∀ a b, p ≤ a → a ≤ b → R a b)
182: 
183: /-- The peak is unique -/
184: theorem single_peaked_peak_unique {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p q : σ}
185:     (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hsq : single_peaked R q) (hne : p ≠ q) : False := by
186:   have hPpq : P R p q := hsp.1 q hne.symm
187:   have hPqp : P R q p := hsq.1 p hne
188:   exact hPpq.2 hPqp.1
189: 
190: /-- Single-peaked implies the peak is the best element (under Reflexive R) -/
191: theorem single_peaked_peak_best {R : σ → σ → Prop} {p : σ} {S : Finset σ}
192:     (hrefl : Reflexive R) (hsp : single_peaked R p) (hpS : p ∈ S) :
193:     is_best_element p S R := by
194:   intro y hy
195:   by_cases heq : y = p
196:   · subst heq; exact hrefl y
197:   · exact (hsp.1 y heq).1
198: 
199: /-- A profile is single-peaked if every voter has single-peaked preferences -/
200: def single_peaked_profile (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ) : Prop :=
201:   ∀ i : ι, single_peaked (prof i).rel (peaks i)
202: 
203: /-! ## Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)
204: 
205: For an odd number of voters with single-peaked preferences over a linearly
206: ordered set, the median peak (the middle element of sorted peaks) is a
207: Condorcet winner under majority rule.
208: 
209: Proof sketch:
210: - For any y < median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak >= median,
211:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
212: - For any y > median: strictly more than n/2 voters have peak <= median,
213:   and by single-peakedness they prefer median to y
214: -/
215: 
216: /-- Sorted list of peaks (with duplicates preserved) -/
217: noncomputable def sorted_peaks_list (peaks : ι → σ) : List σ :=
218:   (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).mergeSort (· ≤ ·)
219: 
220: /-- The median peak: the middle element of sorted peaks.
221:     For odd n, this is the unique middle element. -/
222: noncomputable def median_peak [Inhabited σ] (peaks : ι → σ) : σ :=
223:   let s := sorted_peaks_list peaks
224:   s.getD (s.length / 2) default
225: 
226: /-- **Median Voter Theorem (Black 1948)**: For an odd number of voters with
227:     single-peaked preferences, the median peak is a Condorcet winner. -/
228: theorem median_voter_theorem (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (peaks : ι → σ)

attributes.gen_ai.system_instructions:
[{"type": "text", "content": "Genere des tactiques Lean 4 et soumet-les au verificateur.

OUTILS PRINCIPAUX:
1. get_proof_state() → but courant + lemmes du SearchAgent + plan du Coordinator
2. file_read_lines(start, end) → lit le contexte autour du sorry (declaration, hyps)
3. compile_probe_goal(line) → SI tu as besoin de re-extraire le but (rare)
4. generate_tactics(goal) → suggestions tactiques heuristiques
5. submit_tactic(tactic) → tente UNE tactique complete (fait l'edit + lake build)
6. submit_decomposition(name, goal, sub_proof, main_proof) → introduit `have`
7. compile() → check apres edit (build SUCCESS + sorry delta + axioms)

STRATEGIE D'ATTAQUE (du simple au structurel):
1. Une-shot: rfl, decide, omega, simp, exact <lemme>
2. Adaptation: simp [<lemme>], exact <lemme> _, rw [<lemme>]
3. Combinaison: constructor + sub-tactiques, refine ⟨_, _⟩, cases/induction
4. Decomposition: si la preuve excede 4-5 lignes ou si le but est complexe,
   utilise submit_decomposition pour creer un `have h : sub := by ...` et
   reduire le but principal. PROGRES STRUCTUREL = sorry qui se rapproche
   d'un sous-but plus simple, meme si le compteur ne baisse pas immediatement.

QUAND UTILISER submit_decomposition:
- Conjonctions, equivalences, quantificateurs imbriques
- Buts arithmetiques avec sous-bornes a etablir
- Counting / cardinalite necessitant transfer (Finset → List → countP)
- Quand 2 essais one-shot ont echoue : DECOMPOSE plutot que d'iterer

CYCLE D'ITERATION (si echec):
- Lire l'erreur compilateur (elle est explicite)
- Adapter (ajouter named arg, changer un lemme, ajouter un cast)
- Si 2 adaptations identiques echouent : DECOMPOSER ou demander plus de lemmes
- Si 3 echecs cumules sur le meme sorry : laisser le Critic / Coordinator reformuler

INTERDIT (verifie toujours dans FAILED ATTEMPTS):
- Repeter une tactique deja en echec (meme texte exact)
- Utiliser sorry sur du code metier existant (anti-regression)
- Modifier le theoreme cible ou ses hypotheses pour faire passer la preuve

FIX PATTERNS GENERAUX (heuristiques, pas hard-codes):
- "type mismatch" → caster (norm_cast / push_cast / Nat.cast_*) ou ajouter `show`
- "unsolved goals" → la tactique laisse des sous-buts → enchainer ou decomposer
- "unfold failed" → la def est opaque → utiliser show ou unfold explicite
- "function expected" → arg implicite manquant, ajouter (a := ...) (b := ...)
- "omega failed" → l'expression n'est pas Nat/Int pure → caster d'abord

IMPORTANT — TON OBJECTIF:
Tu construis une preuve INCREMENTALEMENT. Chaque sous-but qui compile est un
gain meme si le sorry global subsiste. Ne te bloque pas en cherchant le coup
parfait : essaie, lis l'erreur, adapte. Le harnais te donne le retour en
quelques secondes."}]

31: variable {ι σ : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ]
32: 
33: /-! ## Margin Function -/
34: 
35: /-- The margin of x over y in a profile:
36:     |{voters strictly preferring x to y}| - |{voters strictly preferring y to x}|
37:     Positive margin means x beats y in pairwise comparison. -/
38: noncomputable def margin (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : ℤ :=
39:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x y) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
40:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel y x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
41:   ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x y) Finset.univ).card : ℤ) -
42:     ((Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel y x) Finset.univ).card : ℤ)
43: 
44: /-- Positive margin: x beats y in pairwise comparison -/
45: def margin_pos (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) : Prop :=
46:   0 < margin prof x y
47: 
48: /-! ## Condorcet Concepts -/
49: 
50: /-- A Condorcet winner: strictly beats every other alternative in pairwise comparison -/
51: def condorcet_winner (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
52:   x ∈ S ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof x y
53: 
54: /-- A Condorcet loser: strictly loses to every other alternative -/
55: def condorcet_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (S : Finset σ) (x : σ) : Prop :=
56:   x ∈ S ∧ S.card ≥ 2 ∧ ∀ y ∈ S, y ≠ x → margin_pos prof y x
57: 
58: /-! ## Social Choice Correspondence -/
59: 
60: /-- A Social Choice Correspondence: maps profiles and alternative sets to chosen alternatives -/
61: def SCC (ι σ : Type*) [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq σ] :=
62:   (ι → PrefOrder σ) → Finset σ → Finset σ
63: 
64: /-! ## Voting Criteria -/
65: 
66: /-- Condorcet criterion: every Condorcet winner is selected -/
67: def condorcet_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
68:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_winner prof S x → x ∈ f prof S
69: 
70: /-- Condorcet loser criterion: no Condorcet loser is selected -/
71: def condorcet_loser_criterion (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
72:   ∀ prof S x, condorcet_loser prof S x → x ∉ f prof S
73: 
74: /-- Pareto efficiency: if all voters prefer x to y, then y is not selected -/
75: def pareto_scc (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
76:   ∀ prof S x y, x ∈ S → y ∈ S →
77:     (∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) → y ∉ f prof S
78: 
79: /-- Monotonicity: improving x's position preserves x's selection -/
80: def monotonicity (f : SCC ι σ) : Prop :=
81:   ∀ prof prof' S x,
82:     x ∈ f prof S → x ∈ S →
83:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, P (prof i).rel x y → P (prof' i).rel x y) →
84:     (∀ i : ι, ∀ y ∈ S, ¬P (prof i).rel y x → ¬P (prof' i).rel y x) →
85:     x ∈ f prof' S
86: 
87: /-! ## Margin Properties -/
88: 
89: theorem margin_antisymm (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x y : σ) :
90:     margin prof x y = - (margin prof y x) := by
91:   unfold margin
92:   ring
93: 
94: theorem margin_self (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) (x : σ) :
95:     margin prof x x = 0 := by
96:   unfold margin
97:   haveI : (i : ι) → Decidable (P (prof i).rel x x) := fun _ => Classical.dec _
98:   have hN : ∀ i : ι, ¬P (prof i).rel x x := fun _ => false_of_P_self
99:   have h_empty : Finset.filter (fun i => P (prof i).rel x x) Finset.univ = ∅ := by
100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi"}]}, {"role": "assistant", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "name": "file_read_lines", "arguments": "{"start":100,"end":250}"}]}, {"role": "tool", "parts": [{"type": "tool_call_response", "id": "call_-7637360048892538059", "response": "100:     ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hN i hi

103: theorem margin_pos_iff_neg_rev (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ} :
104:     margin_pos prof x y ↔ margin prof y x < 0 := by
105:   unfold margin_pos
106:   rw [margin_antisymm prof]

109: /-! ## Condorcet Properties -/
110: 
111: /-- The Condorcet winner is unique (when it exists) -/
112: theorem condorcet_winner_unique (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x y : σ}
113:     (hx : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hy : condorcet_winner prof S y) (hxy : x ≠ y) :
114:     False := by
115:   obtain ⟨hxs, hbeat⟩ := hx
116:   obtain ⟨hys, hbeat'⟩ := hy
117:   have h₁ := hbeat y hys hxy.symm
118:   have h₂ := hbeat' x hxs hxy
119:   unfold margin_pos at h₁ h₂
120:   have := margin_antisymm prof x y
121:   linarith
122: 
123: /-- A Condorcet winner cannot be a Condorcet loser -/
124: theorem condorcet_winner_not_loser (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {S : Finset σ} {x : σ}
125:     (hw : condorcet_winner prof S x) (hl : condorcet_loser prof S x) :
126:     False := by
127:   obtain ⟨hxs, hcard, hlose⟩ := hl
128:   obtain ⟨_, hbeat⟩ := hw
129:   obtain ⟨y, hyS, hxy⟩ := exists_second_distinct_mem (by omega : 2 ≤ S.card) hxs
130:   have h₁ := hbeat y hyS hxy
131:   have h₂ := hlose y hyS hxy

136: /-! ## Unanimity and Margin -/

138: /-- If all voters strictly prefer x to y, the margin is positive -/
139: theorem margin_pos_of_unanimous [Nonempty ι] (prof : ι → PrefOrder σ) {x y : σ}
140:     (h : ∀ i : ι, P (prof i).rel x y) : margin_pos prof x y := by
141:   unfold margin_pos

143:   have hkey : margin prof x y = (Fintype.card ι : ℤ) := by
144:     unfold margin
145:     classical
146:     -- After classical, Decidable instances are uniform via Classical.dec
147:     -- The haveI in margin's definition and our context both resolve to Classical.dec

150:       ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact h i

152:       ext i; simp [Finset.mem_filter]; exact fun hi => hNyx i hi
